message:
test(ffs-harness): pin ext4 i_generation against debugfs

Add a kernel-reference conformance harness that diff-tests
Ext4Inode.generation against debugfs's "Generation: N" output across
seven inode shapes:
  * root directory (inode 2)
  * empty / tiny / multi-block regular files
  * subdirectory
  * fast symlink (target in i_block)
  * extent-mapped symlink (target in data block)

i_generation is the NFS change cookie — silent misreads break NFS
re-export and any cache keyed on (inode, generation). The existing
ext4_inode_flags_uidgid_kernel_reference harness reads mode/uid/gid
from the same inode area but never validated this 32-bit field.

Cross-corpus invariants: at least 5 data inodes must be exercised
(beyond root), and all per-case comparisons assert byte-for-byte
equality. (A "at least one non-zero generation" pin is unavailable
because debugfs allocates inodes with deterministic zero generations
rather than the random ones the kernel mints; the per-case equality
still catches a parser that drops or zeroes the field.)
